critic_round1_raw: Second pass. The design mentions reviewing attention weights and SHAP explanations to detect "implausible reliance on vasopressor orders or other late signals already indicating sepsis." If vasopressor orders occur AFTER the prediction anchor (onset - 6 hours), they cannot be in the feature set. However, if a vasopressor order was placed as part of the pre-diagnosis sepsis workup before the formal SOFA criteria were met, it could appear in the pre-anchor feature window. Is this a leakage concern?
defender_round1_raw: The design explicitly addresses this: "Model explanations (SHAP, attention visualization) are reviewed by clinicians to detect implausible reliance on vasopressor orders or other late signals already indicating sepsis." This shows awareness of the concern. More importantly, the label definition ties onset to the FIRST TIME both infection criteria AND organ dysfunction criteria are simultaneously met. A vasopressor order placed as part of a sepsis workup before SOFA>=2 would appear before the onset anchor and would be a legitimate predictor. A vasopressor placed after onset (i.e., in the 0-6 hour window before the anchor) would be excluded by the anchor time cutoff. The design correctly handles this.
critic_round2_raw: Accepting: the strict anchor time cutoff prevents post-onset vasopressor orders from appearing in features. The clinician review of attention weights is an additional safeguard. No leakage concern.
defender_round2_raw: Correct. The anchor-based feature cutoff is the primary protection; clinician review is secondary validation.
adjudication_raw: Run 2: All concerns resolved. Verdict: defense_wins.
